title: Generating labeled training images for use in training a computational neural network for object or action recognition
abstract: A system for training a computational neural network to recognise objects and/or actions from images, the system comprising: a training unit, comprising: an input interface configured to receive: a plurality of images captured from one or more cameras, each image having an associated timestamp indicating the time the image was captured; a data stream containing a plurality of timestamps each associated with an object and/or action; the data stream being generated by a system in an operative field of view of the one or more cameras; an image identification unit configured to identify from the plurality of images a set of images that each have a timestamp that correlates to a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream; a data-labelling unit configured to determine, for each image of the set of images, an image label that indicates the probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action in dependence on the correlation between the timestamp for the image and the timestamp associated with the object and/or action from the data stream; and an output interface configured to output the image labels for use in training a computational neural network to identify from images objects of the object classes and/or the specified actions.
claims:
Claims 1 . A system for training a computational neural network to recognise objects and/or actions from images, the system comprising: a training unit, comprising: an input interface configured to receive: a plurality of images captured from one or more cameras, each image having an associated timestamp indicating the time the image was captured; a data stream containing a plurality of timestamps each associated with an object and/or action; the data stream being generated by a system in an operative field of view of the one or more cameras; an image identification unit configured to identify from the plurality of images a set of images that each have a timestamp that correlates to a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream; a data-labelling unit configured to determine, for each image of the set of images, an image label that indicates the probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action in dependence on the correlation between the timestamp for the image and the timestamp associated with the object and/or action from the data stream; and an output interface configured to output the image labels for use in training a computational neural network to identify from images objects of the object classes and/or the specified actions. 2. A system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein image-identification unit comprises a synchronising unit configured to synchronise the timestamps associated with the images with the timestamps associated with the objects and/or actions from the data stream. 3. A system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein each image of the set of images used to train the computational neural network has a timestamp within a specified time interval of a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream. 4. A system as claimed in any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the image identification unit is configured to identify a set of primary images each having an associated 

 timestamp within a specified time interval of a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream. 5. A system as claimed in claim 4, wherein the data-labelling unit is configured to assign, to each primary image, a probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action. 6. A system as claimed in claim 4 or 5, wherein the image identification unit is further configured to identify, for each primary image, a subset of secondary images having a timestamp within a specified time interval of the timestamp for the primary image. 7. A system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the data labelling unit is further configured to assign, for each subset of secondary images, a probability to each image in the subset that the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action determined from the probabilities assigned to the primary image for that subset. 8. A system as claimed in claim 7, wherein the data labelling unit is configured to assign the probabilities to each image in the subset of secondary images further from the difference between the timestamp of the secondary image and the timestamp of the primary image for the subset. 9. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein: the data stream contains information on a plurality of objects and timestamps associated with each object; each of the plurality of objects is depicted in at least one image of the plurality of received images; and the set of identified images each have timestamps that correlate to timestamps associated with an object from the data stream. 10. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, the training unit further comprising a categorising unit configured to categorise each object into an object class of the set of object classes using the information in the data stream on that object, and wherein the image label for each image of the set of images form a classification label indicating a probability for each class that the image depicts an object of that class. 1 1 . A system as claimed in claim 10, wherein the data labelling unit is configured to determine the classification label for each image from the categorisation of the object having a timestamp in the data stream that correlates to the timestamp for that image. 12. A system as claimed in claim 10 or 1 1 , when dependent on claim 4, wherein the data labelling unit is configured to assign, to each primary image, a first probability that the image depicts an object of the object class into which the object having a timestamp within the first specified time interval of the image has been categorised. 13. A system as claimed in claim 12, when dependent on claim 6, wherein the data labelling unit is configured to, for each subset of secondary images, assign to each image in the subset a probability that the image depicts an object of the same object class as the primary image for that subset. 14. A system as claimed in any of claims 10 to 13, wherein the system further comprises an object-recognition unit adapted to use a computational neural network to identify objects from images and configured to: receive the set of images and the image labels output from the training unit; and use the set of images and their image labels to train the computational neural network to identify from images objects belonging to the object classes. 15. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the image label for each image in the set of images comprises a value indicating a probability that the image depicts a person performing the specified action. 16. A system as claimed in claim 15, when dependent on claim 4, wherein the data labelling unit is configured to assign, to each primary image, a first probability that the image depicts a person performing a specified action. 17. A system as claimed in claim 16, when dependent on claim 6, wherein the data labelling unit is configured to, for each subset of secondary images, assign to each 

 image in the subset a probability that the image depicts the same specified action as the primary image for that subset. 18. A system as claimed in claim 16 or 17, wherein the system further comprises an image-data labelling unit adapted to use a computational neural network to identify actions depicted in images and configured to: receive the set of images and the image labels output from the training unit; and use the set of images and their image labels to train the computational neural network to identify from images the specified actions. 19. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the system comprises a data-generating system configured to generate the data stream, wherein the data- generating system is in an operative field of view of the one or more cameras. 20. A system as claimed in claim 19, wherein the data-generating system is a point- of-sale system, and the information on the object in each data set is point-of-sale (POS) data for the object. 21 . A method of training a computational neural network used by an object and/or action recognition system, the method comprising: receiving from one or more cameras a plurality of images each having an associated timestamp indicating the time the image was captured; receiving a data stream containing a plurality of timestamps each associated with an object and/or action, the data stream being generated by a system in an operative field of view of the one or more cameras; identifying from the plurality of images a set of images that each have a timestamp that correlates to a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream; determining, for each image of the set of images, an image label that indicates the probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action in dependence on the correlation between the timestamp for the image and the timestamp associated with the object and/or action from the data stream. 22. A method as claimed in claim 21 , further comprising using the set of images and their labels to train the computational neural network to identify from images objects of the object classes and/or the specified actions. 23. A method as claimed in claim 21 or 22, wherein the method further comprises synchronising the timestamps associated with the images with the timestamps associated with the objects and/or actions from the data stream. 24. A method as claimed in claim 23, wherein each image of the set of images used to train the computational neural network has a timestamp within a specified time interval of a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream. 25. A method as claimed in any of claims 21 to 24, wherein the step of identifying the set of images comprises identifying a set of primary images each having an associated timestamp within a first specified time interval of a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream. 26. A method as claimed in claim 25, wherein the step of determining the image label for each image of the set of images comprises, for each primary image: assigning to the image a probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action. 27. A method as claimed in claim 25 or 26, wherein the step of identifying the set of images further comprises, for each primary image, identifying a subset of secondary images having a timestamp within a second specified time interval of the timestamp for the primary image. 28. A method as claimed in claim 27, wherein the step of determining the image label for each image of the set of images further comprises, for each subset of secondary images, assigning to each image in the subset a probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action determined from the probabilities assigned to the primary image for that subset. 29. A method as claimed in claim 28, wherein the probabilities assigned to each image in the subset of secondary images is further determined from the difference between the timestamp of the secondary image and the timestamp of the primary image for the subset. 30. A method as claimed in any of claims 21 to 29, wherein: the data stream contains information on a plurality of objects and timestamps associated with each object; each of the plurality of objects is depicted in at least one image of the plurality of images; and the set of identified images each have timestamps that correlate to a timestamps associated with an object from the data stream. 31 . A method as claimed in claim 30, the method further comprising categorising each object into an object class of the set of object classes using the information in the data stream on that object, and wherein the image label for each image of the set of images forms a classification label indicating a probability for each class that the image depicts an object of that class. 32. A method as claimed in claim 31 , wherein the classification label for each image is determined from the categorisation of the object having a timestamp in the data stream that correlates to the timestamp for that image. 33. A method as claimed in claim 31 or 32, when dependent on claim 25, wherein the step of determining the classification labels comprises, for each primary image: assigning to the image a first probability that the image depicts an object of the object class into which the object having a timestamp within the first specified time interval of the image has been categorised. 34. A method as claimed in claim 33, when dependent on claim 27, wherein the step of determining the classification labels further comprises, for each subset of secondary images, assigning to each image in the subset a probability that the image depicts an object of the same object class as the primary image for that subset. 35. A method as claimed in any of claims 21 to 34, wherein the image label for each image in the set of images comprises a value indicating a probability that the image depicts a person performing the specified action. 36. A method as claimed in claim 35, when dependent on claim 25, wherein the step of determining the image labels comprises, for each primary image: assigning to the image a first probability that the image depicts a person performing the specified action. 37. A method as claimed in claim 36, when dependent on claim 27, wherein the step of determining the image labels further comprises, for each subset of secondary images, assigning to each image in the subset a probability that the image depicts the same specified action as the primary image for that subset. 38. A method as claimed in any of claims 21 to 37, wherein the data stream is generated independently of the plurality of images. 39. A method as claimed in any of claims 21 to 38, wherein the data stream is generated by a point-of-sale system in view of the camera used to capture the plurality of images, and the information on the object in each data set is point-of-sale (POS) data for the object. 40. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium having stored thereon program instructions that, when executed at a computer system, cause the computer system to perform a method of training a computational neural network to identify from images objects and/or actions, the method comprising: receiving from one or more cameras a plurality of images each having an associated timestamp indicating the time the image was captured; receiving a data stream containing a plurality of timing indications each associated with an object and/or action; identifying from the plurality of images a set of images that each have a timestamp that correlates to a timestamp associated with an object and/or action from the data stream; 

 determining, for each image of the set of images, an image label that indicates the probability the image depicts: (i) an object of each of a set of one or more specified object classes; and/or (ii) a specified human action in dependence on the correlation between the timestamp for the image and the timestamp associated with the object and/or action from the data stream. 41 . Computer readable code configured to perform the steps of the method of any of claims 21 to 39 when the code is run on a computer.
